arguments.title: Executive Compensation Changes — FY2025 Plan
arguments.body:
Executive Compensation Changes — FY2025 Plan

This communication outlines updates to the company's executive compensation framework for fiscal 2025. The changes are designed to ensure alignment between long-term value creation, sustainable growth, and our commitments to privacy, security, and environmental stewardship. The Board has approved adjustments which shift a larger portion of target compensation into long-term equity awards tied to multi-year strategic milestones, including product innovation targets and services growth metrics.

Key elements include a renewed emphasis on multi-year performance measures that reflect total shareholder returns, strategic R&D milestones (especially progress in Apple Silicon roadmaps such as the next-generation M-series workstreams), and services revenue expansion. The Board also reaffirmed the importance of operational excellence across retail, supply chain, and compliance functions, and expects executive leaders to cascade these expectations into their organizations.

What leaders should do now: review team objectives to ensure they support the updated metrics; coordinate with People & Inclusion and Finance on communication timing; and ensure any external disclosures follow investor-relations guidance. We expect HR partners to issue individual award letters and managers to schedule one-on-one discussions with impacted executives. Questions about timelines and measurement details should be routed to Compensation & Benefits and the Legal team.

These changes reflect a continued focus on rewarding outcomes that reinforce long-term customer value, privacy-first innovation, and responsible supply chain practices. Management will provide additional guidance on milestone definitions and measurement approaches in the coming weeks. Thank you for your leadership in translating these directives into operational plans that support our mission.

arguments.title: Data Governance Framework Update
arguments.body:
Data Governance Framework Update

This article summarizes the enhancements to our Data Governance Framework, intended to strengthen stewardship of customer and employee data across products and enterprise systems. The update clarifies roles, introduces new data classification tiers, and codifies retention and minimization principles. These changes support our privacy commitments while enabling responsible product analytics and machine learning initiatives.

Highlights of the update include stricter controls on high-risk datasets, mandatory data-use approvals for cross-functional projects, and a simplified process for requesting dataset access. Data stewards will now be responsible for maintaining inventories and approving access requests. The framework also prescribes encryption and handling standards for data in transit and at rest, consistent with our security policies.

Managers should review team workflows to identify any dependencies on affected datasets and schedule reviews with IT & Security and Legal to ensure ongoing compliance. Training modules will be made available in the Learning portal and mandatory completion may be required for teams handling sensitive data. For product teams, the framework includes guidance on privacy-preserving ML approaches and telemetry minimization to support Apple’s privacy-first values.

Questions about classification or approvals should be directed to the Data Governance Office. We will publish a detailed change-log and FAQs on the Leadership & Governance channel and follow up with stakeholder briefings as needed.

arguments.title: All-Hands Recap — Inclusion Initiatives
arguments.body:
All-Hands Recap — Inclusion Initiatives

Last week’s All-Hands featured a series of updates on our inclusion initiatives, highlighting progress on representation goals, mentorship programs, and partnerships with community organizations. Leaders across functions shared results from targeted hiring efforts, mentoring successes, and new learning opportunities aimed at fostering a more inclusive workplace.

Key takeaways include measurable progress in early-career programs, expanded parental-leave support, and the launch of several employee-resource-group-led workshops that focus on inclusive leadership and psychological safety. The People & Inclusion team also announced a refreshed set of recruitment practices to reduce bias in screening and interviewing processes.

Employees are encouraged to participate in upcoming mentorship cohorts and to engage with ERG events listed on the People & Inclusion pages. Managers should ensure teams have time to attend key workshops and incorporate inclusive behaviors into performance conversations. Additionally, regional initiatives will adapt global programs to local markets, and local HR partners will share timelines and registration details.

For those who missed the All-Hands, the recording and slide deck are available on the People & Inclusion channel, along with resources on how to nominate mentors and mentees. We appreciate your ongoing commitment to building a workplace where diverse perspectives are respected and where every employee can thrive.

arguments.title: Parental Leave Policy Update
arguments.body:
Parental Leave Policy Update

We are pleased to announce enhancements to the parental leave policy designed to support families across the company. The updated policy increases paid leave duration for primary and secondary caregivers, simplifies eligibility rules, and introduces flexible return-to-work options to support a smoother transition back to the workplace.

The policy changes include extended paid leave for primary caregivers, an expanded definition of eligible family members, and a new phased-return program that allows for part-time schedules or reduced hours during the reintegration period. These changes aim to promote retention, support career continuity, and acknowledge the diverse caregiving responsibilities across our global employee base.

Managers should coordinate with People & Inclusion and HR to understand the operational implications and plan coverage during leaves. Employees planning parental leave should consult the People portal for steps to submit requests, and HR will provide individualized support for planning and benefits navigation.

We recognize that caregiving needs vary widely; as such, managers are encouraged to work with HR partners to find equitable arrangements that meet business needs while supporting employee well-being. If you have questions, reach out to the People & Inclusion team via the People & Inclusion channel.

arguments.title: Career Development Pathways
arguments.body:
Career Development Pathways

Apple continues to invest in clear career pathways that help employees plan and progress. The new Career Development Pathways initiative provides role-specific competencies, suggested learning plans, and mentorship opportunities to support internal mobility and professional growth.

The program includes updated role families, mapping of skills to levels, and recommended training modules available through Learning. Employees and managers can use the self-service toolkit to identify skill gaps, plan stretch assignments, and find mentors who align with career goals. The pathways emphasize cross-functional exposure, enabling engineers, designers, product managers, and retail staff to access rotational experiences.

People & Inclusion encourages managers to incorporate development discussions into regular one-on-ones and to leverage internal job postings to support mobility. Success stories will be showcased on the People & Inclusion channel to highlight pathways from entry-level roles into leadership positions.

For next steps, employees should review their role family documentation, enroll in recommended learning plans, and discuss opportunities with their managers. HR business partners are available to assist with individual career planning and rotational program enrollment.

arguments.title: M4 Chip Roadmap & R&D Priorities
arguments.body:
M4 Chip Roadmap & R&D Priorities

This article provides an overview of our chip roadmap and R&D priorities related to the next-generation M-series silicon. Our engineering teams are focused on performance-per-watt improvements, heterogeneous compute integration, and tooling that accelerates developer adoption across macOS and iPadOS products.

Key priorities include optimizing neural-engine throughput for on-device ML workloads, reducing power draw for sustained workloads in portable devices, and improving compiler toolchains to ease porting of performance-critical code. Cross-team collaboration between silicon design, firmware, and OS teams remains essential to meet our multi-year targets.

Product teams should examine how upcoming silicon capabilities influence roadmap decisions for professional applications, developer tools, and services that rely on on-device inference. The Product Innovation channel hosts technical notes, benchmarks, and timelines that engineering leads can use to plan product integration and validation efforts.

Requests for early silicon samples and testing slots can be placed through the Product Innovation pages. Engineering managers are encouraged to align test plans with R&D milestones and to coordinate thermal and reliability validation with systems teams. We appreciate the continued dedication of our chipset and systems teams to advancing platform leadership.

arguments.title: visionOS App Submission Guidelines
arguments.body:
visionOS App Submission Guidelines

As visionOS adoption grows, we have published updated app submission guidelines to ensure high-quality, accessible spatial experiences. The guidelines cover UI patterns, performance budgets, privacy disclosures, and testing recommendations for spatial input and eye-tracking interactions.

Developers should pay particular attention to rendering budgets and motion-sickness mitigations, use of native spatial primitives, and guidelines for audio and haptics to create comfortable experiences. Accessibility remains a priority; include assistive-navigation options and ensure content is perceivable across device configurations.

The Product Innovation channel includes a checklist for review readiness and sample projects demonstrating best practices. Teams planning a public or internal deployment should coordinate with the Platform Partner team for compatibility testing and performance profiling.

For early access to device labs or submission support, submit requests via the Product Innovation pages. The Platform team will provide review timelines and feedback to help teams meet store acceptance criteria and deliver polished spatial apps.

arguments.title: AI Safety Best Practices
arguments.body:
AI Safety Best Practices

In line with our commitment to privacy-preserving and responsible AI, this article outlines recommended practices for building safe on-device and cloud-assisted models. The guidance covers data minimization, guardrails for user-facing outputs, and techniques for model evaluation and monitoring that align with our privacy and security obligations.

Teams should favor on-device inference where feasible, apply differential privacy or federated learning approaches for telemetry collection, and implement robust logging and red-teaming for generative outputs. Evaluation metrics should include fairness, robustness, and explainability measures alongside traditional accuracy metrics.

Product Innovation maintains a repository of evaluation toolkits, test benchmarks, and incident escalation paths for potential model-related harms. Legal and Security must be engaged early for projects involving sensitive domains or external data sharing.

Adopting these practices helps ensure that AI-enabled features enhance user value while maintaining Apple's standards for safety and privacy. Reach out to the AI program office via the Product Innovation channel for consultation and review support.

arguments.title: Hardware Prototyping Lab Access
arguments.body:
Hardware Prototyping Lab Access

The Hardware Prototyping Lab provides teams with access to tools, bench space, and prototype hardware required for early-stage validation. This announcement details booking procedures, lab safety rules, and the prioritization policy for prototype allocation during peak development windows.

To request access, teams should submit a booking request on the Product Innovation pages, including a brief scope statement and test requirements. Safety training is mandatory for first-time users and includes handling of sensitive components, ESD protocols, and secure disposal procedures for test materials.

Lab prioritization favors projects aligned with near-term product milestones and cross-functional initiatives that require integrated validation. The lab team will coordinate with managers to schedule on-device testing and will publish queue times to help teams plan integration activities.

For special test setups or long-duration experiments, contact the lab managers through the Product Innovation channel to discuss resourcing and scheduling. We appreciate teams following lab guidelines to maintain a safe and productive environment for hardware innovation.

arguments.title: Holiday Launch Staffing Playbook
arguments.body:
Holiday Launch Staffing Playbook

With the holiday season approaching, Retail & Stores teams should review the staffing playbook to ensure peak readiness. This playbook outlines scheduling best practices, cross-training recommendations, and customer-flow strategies that have proven effective during recent large-scale product launches.

Key recommendations include proactive staffing templates for front-of-store and Genius Bar, surge staffing plans for high-traffic days, and scripts to support customer education about new product features and services. The playbook also highlights coordination points with Marketing and Inventory teams to align promotions and stock levels.

Regional retail leads should use the Pages tab to access localized templates and to submit staffing requests. Managers are encouraged to prioritize cross-training so that Specialists can flex between customer service and product demonstrations during surges.

Finally, the Retail & Stores channel contains playbook checklists, sample schedules, and training modules to prepare teams for a smooth customer experience during the busiest season of the year.

arguments.title: Genius Bar Self-Service Updates
arguments.body:
Genius Bar Self-Service Updates

We’ve rolled out updates to Genius Bar self-service tools to reduce repair cycle times and improve customer experience. Improvements include revised diagnostics workflows, enhanced remote troubleshooting scripts, and optimized parts inventory handling to accelerate common repairs.

Technicians should review the updated troubleshooting guides and system workflows posted on the Retail & Stores Pages. The new flows emphasize first-contact resolutions and provide escalation paths for less common hardware issues. Training modules will be available to ensure consistent application of the new processes.

Store managers should adjust scheduling to allow time for technicians to complete training and to incorporate updated repair time estimates into customer bookings. For high-volume repair types, consider temporary shift adjustments to minimize customer wait times.

Feedback on the updated tools is welcome; please post operational observations on the Retail & Stores channel so the support team can iterate on tooling and documentation.

arguments.title: Retail Store Safety Protocols
arguments.body:
Retail Store Safety Protocols

Our top priority is the safety of employees and customers. This article outlines updated safety protocols for in-store operations, including guidance on crowd management during peak launches, emergency response coordination, and adjustments to store floor layouts to improve traffic flow.

The protocols recommend pre-event briefings, designated safety leads per store, and clear signage to help manage lines and capacity. For stores hosting high-profile product events, coordinate with regional operations to request additional support staff and to confirm local emergency contact procedures.

All store teams must complete the updated safety training on the Retail & Stores Pages and ensure emergency kits and first-aid supplies are stocked and accessible. Managers should report any safety incidents promptly and document follow-up actions to reduce recurrence.

Local store teams may have additional requirements; consult regional leads for localized guidance. Maintaining these protocols helps ensure a safe shopping experience for customers and a secure workplace for our teams.

arguments.title: Supplier Responsibility Audit Results
arguments.body:
Supplier Responsibility Audit Results

The latest supplier responsibility audits highlight progress and areas for continued focus across environmental, labor, and health & safety practices. The results reflect improvements in audit scores for several key suppliers and identify targeted corrective-action plans for others.

Highlights include measurable reductions in emissions for select manufacturing sites, strengthened worker-safety training programs, and improved traceability for critical materials. The Supply Chain & Manufacturing channel hosts the detailed audit reports and supplier-specific action plans for relevant teams.

Procurement and engineering partners should review supplier action plans and collaborate on implementation timelines. Where audits identify significant risks, teams are expected to prioritize mitigation and communicate impacts to program managers and compliance officers.

We continue to work with suppliers to elevate standards and ensure alignment with our sustainability goals. Questions about specific audits or corrective measures should be directed to Supplier Responsibility contacts listed on the channel pages.

arguments.title: Manufacturing Expansion: India Update
arguments.body:
Manufacturing Expansion: India Update

This update covers progress in expanding manufacturing capacity in India, including investments in local supplier development, facility upgrades, and workforce training programs. The initiative aims to diversify manufacturing footprints while supporting regional economic development and resilience.

Key milestones include onboarding new suppliers, ramping pilot production lines, and establishing quality-assurance frameworks consistent with global standards. The Supply Chain & Manufacturing channel hosts detailed timelines and contacts for teams coordinating regional sourcing and logistics.

Project leads should review localization requirements, parts qualification schedules, and regulatory considerations that affect timelines. Cross-functional coordination with Product, Quality, and Regulatory teams remains critical to meet launch windows while maintaining product integrity.

We appreciate the work of teams supporting this expansion and encourage questions to be routed through the channel pages for transparency and collaboration across regions.

arguments.title: Sustainability in Procurement
arguments.body:
Sustainability in Procurement

Procurement continues to integrate sustainability criteria into supplier selection and contract terms. This article outlines updated scoring factors that prioritize reduced carbon footprints, recycled-material content, and supplier commitments to circular-economy practices.

Teams should incorporate the sustainability checklist into sourcing evaluations and track supplier progress against published targets. The Supply Chain & Manufacturing channel contains tools and templates to support supplier assessments and to document sustainability commitments in vendor agreements.

Procurement partners are encouraged to engage with Supplier Responsibility teams to align contractual requirements with our 2030 goals and to ensure transparent reporting of progress and challenges.

By embedding sustainability into procurement decisions, we reinforce our commitment to responsible manufacturing and product lifecycle stewardship across our supply chain.

arguments.title: Privacy Policy Update — Data Minimization
arguments.body:
Privacy Policy Update — Data Minimization

We have published an update to the privacy policy emphasizing data minimization and clearer disclosures for telemetry collection. This update aligns product teams and analytics functions with a stricter standard for collecting only data necessary for product functionality and safety.

Beginning immediately, teams must document the specific purpose for any telemetry they collect and identify lifecycle retention periods. High-risk telemetry requires additional approvals and may be subject to anonymization or aggregation requirements before analysis.

This update is required reading for all teams handling product or customer data. Please complete the acknowledgement on this article to confirm you have read and understand the changes. Legal will provide guidance on compliance and timelines for implementation across affected systems.

arguments.title: EU DMA Readiness — Team Actions
arguments.body:
EU DMA Readiness — Team Actions

The EU Digital Markets Act introduces obligations that impact platform interoperability, gatekeeper responsibilities, and certain distribution practices. This article summarizes immediate actions teams must take to support readiness and compliance across impacted products and services.

Action items include inventorying impacted integrations, assessing technical changes required for compatibility, and coordinating with Legal & Compliance on public disclosures. Product managers must identify dependencies on third-party integrations and timeline impacts for roadmap adjustments.

This article is mandatory reading for teams working on platform features that interact with third-party services. Please acknowledge this article to confirm receipt; Legal will follow up with role-specific guidance and implementation checkpoints.

arguments.title: Export Controls & Compliance — Mandatory Read
arguments.body:
Export Controls & Compliance — Mandatory Read

Changes to export-control regulations require updated compliance workflows for hardware and software transfers that may involve restricted technologies. This article details the updated matrix for classification, licensing requirements, and escalation paths for potential controlled-technology transfers.

Teams handling international shipments, data transfers, or partner integrations should review the guidance and complete the acknowledgement on this article. Legal & Compliance will provide case-specific consultations for any transactions that may trigger licensing requirements.

Follow the prescribed escalation paths for urgent matters, and coordinate with Supply Chain & Manufacturing for export documentation and with Product teams for classification inquiries. Completion of the read-confirmation for this article is required for teams with export-control exposure.

arguments.title: MDM Enrollment Changes & Device Policy
arguments.body:
MDM Enrollment Changes & Device Policy

IT has updated the MDM enrollment flows and revised device policy to align with new security baselines. The changes streamline enrollment for corporate devices, tighten configuration controls, and introduce updated enforcement for device posture checks.

Teams should review new enrollment steps, ensure corporate devices are re-enrolled where required, and confirm their access to essential enterprise apps post-enrollment. The IT & Security channel provides step-by-step guides and troubleshooting resources.

Device owners who encounter issues should contact the IT helpdesk; managers are asked to coordinate device refresh timelines to minimize disruption. These updates are part of broader efforts to improve device security and to enable secure access to internal tools and data.

arguments.title: Incident Response Playbook
arguments.body:
Incident Response Playbook

This playbook consolidates incident-response procedures for IT & Security teams, covering detection, containment, eradication, and recovery phases. It provides clear escalation paths, communication templates, and guidance on stakeholder notifications for incidents affecting devices, networks, or cloud services.

Teams should use the playbook to standardize initial triage steps, preserve evidence, and notify the Security Operations Center for high-severity incidents. The document also outlines roles and responsibilities for cross-functional coordination with Legal, Communications, and Product teams during an incident.

Managers must ensure on-call rosters are current and that relevant staff have access to the playbook and runbooks. We will conduct tabletop exercises periodically to validate preparedness and refine procedures based on lessons learned.

arguments.title: Passwordless Rollout Timeline
arguments.body:
Passwordless Rollout Timeline

IT is initiating a phased rollout of passwordless authentication across corporate applications to enhance security and reduce friction. The timeline outlines pilot cohorts, enrollment windows, and support resources for teams transitioning from password-based access.

Pilot teams will begin enrollment next quarter with broader availability following successful validation. The rollout leverages platform authentication standards and requires device posture checks to ensure secure access. Training and self-help materials will be available on the IT & Security channel.

Managers should identify pilot candidates and plan for enrollment windows that minimize operational disruption. Feedback from pilot groups will inform subsequent rollouts and the final support model for enterprise users.

arguments.title: Third-Party Tool Approval Process
arguments.body:
Third-Party Tool Approval Process

This article details the approval workflow for third-party developer tools and SaaS services used by product and operations teams. The process includes security review, data-flow mapping, and contractual requirements to ensure vendor compliance with corporate policies.

Teams proposing new tools should complete the intake form and provide information on data access, integration points, and business justification. Security and Legal will coordinate reviews and publish approval decisions. Emergency procurement paths exist for critical business needs but require immediate post-hoc review.

Adhering to the approval process helps manage risk and ensures consistent protection for company and user data. For questions, reach out to IT & Security via the channel and consult the intake form on the Pages tab.
